description: References non-existent column o.orderDate instead of o.created_at
question: How many orders were placed in each city this month?
objective: Join orders and users. Filter o.created_at for current month using DATE_TRUNC. GROUP BY u.city. COUNT o.order_id.
sql: SELECT u.city, COUNT(o.order_id) AS order_count FROM orders o JOIN users u ON o.user_id = u.user_id WHERE o.orderDate >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) GROUP BY u.city ORDER BY order_count DESC
table_schema:
Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)
Table: users (user_id PK, full_name VARCHAR, email VARCHAR, city VARCHAR, status VARCHAR)

description: References non-existent column o.total_amount instead of o.grand_total
question: What is the total revenue this month?
objective: SUM(o.grand_total) from orders where created_at is in current month.
sql: SELECT SUM(o.total_amount) AS total_revenue FROM orders o WHERE o.created_at >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
table_schema: Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, subtotal NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)

description: References non-existent column p.product_name instead of p.name
question: What are the top 5 products by revenue?
objective: Join order_items, product_variants, products. GROUP BY p.name. SUM(oi.total_price). ORDER BY DESC. LIMIT 5.
sql: SELECT p.product_name, SUM(oi.total_price) AS revenue FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.product_name ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 5
table_schema:
Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)
Table: order_items (item_id PK, order_id FK->orders, variant_id FK->product_variants, quantity INT, unit_price NUMERIC, total_price NUMERIC)
Table: product_variants (variant_id PK, product_id FK->products, sku VARCHAR, price NUMERIC)
Table: products (product_id PK, category_id FK->categories, name VARCHAR, base_price NUMERIC, status VARCHAR)

description: References non-existent column oi.price instead of oi.total_price
question: What is the total revenue by category?
objective: Join order_items, product_variants, products, categories. SUM(oi.total_price). GROUP BY c.name.
sql: SELECT c.name AS category, SUM(oi.price) AS total_revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id GROUP BY c.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
table_schema:
Table: order_items (item_id PK, order_id FK->orders, variant_id FK->product_variants, quantity INT, unit_price NUMERIC, total_price NUMERIC)
Table: product_variants (variant_id PK, product_id FK->products, sku VARCHAR, price NUMERIC)
Table: products (product_id PK, category_id FK->categories, name VARCHAR, status VARCHAR)
Table: categories (category_id PK, name VARCHAR, slug VARCHAR)

description: References non-existent column u.username instead of u.full_name
question: Show the top 10 customers by total spend
objective: Join orders and users. GROUP BY u.full_name. SUM(o.grand_total). ORDER BY DESC. LIMIT 10.
sql: SELECT u.username, SUM(o.grand_total) AS total_spend FROM orders o JOIN users u ON o.user_id = u.user_id GROUP BY u.username ORDER BY total_spend DESC LIMIT 10
table_schema:
Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)
Table: users (user_id PK, full_name VARCHAR, email VARCHAR, city VARCHAR, status VARCHAR)

description: order_items joined directly to products on oi.product_id — skips required product_variants bridge
question: What are the top 5 products by units sold?
objective: Join order_items to product_variants to products. GROUP BY p.name. SUM(oi.quantity). LIMIT 5.
sql: SELECT p.name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS units_sold FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.name ORDER BY units_sold DESC LIMIT 5
table_schema:
Table: order_items (item_id PK, order_id FK->orders, variant_id FK->product_variants, quantity INT, unit_price NUMERIC, total_price NUMERIC)
Table: product_variants (variant_id PK, product_id FK->products, sku VARCHAR, price NUMERIC)
Table: products (product_id PK, category_id FK->categories, name VARCHAR, status VARCHAR)

description: orders joined directly to categories on o.category_id — skips order_items, product_variants, products
question: What is the total revenue by product category?
objective: Join orders -> order_items -> product_variants -> products -> categories. SUM(oi.total_price). GROUP BY c.name.
sql: SELECT c.name AS category, SUM(o.grand_total) AS revenue FROM orders o JOIN categories c ON o.category_id = c.category_id GROUP BY c.name ORDER BY revenue DESC
table_schema:

description: orders joined directly to brands on o.brand_id — skips order_items, product_variants, products
question: Which brands generated the most revenue last month?
objective: Join orders -> order_items -> product_variants -> products -> brands. SUM(oi.total_price). GROUP BY b.name. Filter last 30 days.
sql: SELECT b.name AS brand, SUM(o.grand_total) AS revenue FROM orders o JOIN brands b ON o.brand_id = b.brand_id WHERE o.created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY b.name ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 10

question: What is the revenue per category this quarter?
objective: Join order_items -> product_variants -> products -> categories. SUM(oi.total_price). GROUP BY c.name.

description: products joined directly to orders on o.product_id — skips order_items and product_variants
question: How many orders included Electronics products?
objective: Join orders -> order_items -> product_variants -> products -> categories. COUNT DISTINCT o.order_id. Filter c.name = 'Electronics'.
sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS order_count FROM orders o JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.product_id JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id WHERE c.name = 'Electronics'

question: What is the total revenue contribution per brand?
objective: Join orders -> order_items -> product_variants -> products -> brands. SUM(oi.total_price). GROUP BY b.name.
sql: SELECT b.name AS brand, AVG(o.grand_total) AS avg_revenue FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id JOIN brands b ON p.brand_id = b.brand_id GROUP BY b.name ORDER BY avg_revenue DESC

question: What is the revenue contribution of each city per product category?
objective: Join orders, users, order_items, product_variants, products, categories. SUM(oi.total_price). GROUP BY u.city, c.name.
sql: SELECT u.city, c.name AS category, SUM(o.grand_total) AS revenue FROM orders o JOIN users u ON o.user_id = u.user_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id GROUP BY u.city, c.name ORDER BY revenue DESC

description: Date filter uses o.order_date — non-existent column, correct column is o.created_at
question: How many orders were placed in the last 30 days?
objective: COUNT order_ids from orders where o.created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'.
sql: SELECT COUNT(order_id) AS order_count FROM orders WHERE order_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'
table_schema: Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)

description: Date filter uses MySQL MONTH()/YEAR() functions — invalid PostgreSQL syntax, should use DATE_TRUNC
question: What is the total revenue this month?
objective: SUM(o.grand_total) from orders where DATE_TRUNC('month', o.created_at) = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE).
sql: SELECT SUM(grand_total) AS total_revenue FROM orders WHERE MONTH(created_at) = MONTH(NOW()) AND YEAR(created_at) = YEAR(NOW())
table_schema: Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)

description: Non-overlapping Q1 vs Q2 windows share March — Q1 ends 2024-03-31 but Q2 starts 2024-03-01
question: Compare total orders in Q1 vs Q2 this year
objective: Two subqueries: Q1 covers Jan 1 to Mar 31, Q2 covers Apr 1 to Jun 30. Both windows must be non-overlapping.
sql:
SELECT
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE created_at BETWEEN '2024-01-01' AND '2024-03-31') AS q1_orders,
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE created_at BETWEEN '2024-03-01' AND '2024-06-30') AS q2_orders
table_schema: Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)

description: Date filter uses o.date — non-existent column, correct column is o.created_at
question: Show weekly revenue for the last 3 months
objective: DATE_TRUNC o.created_at to week. SUM(o.grand_total). Filter o.created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '3 months'. GROUP BY week.
sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('week', o.date) AS week, SUM(o.grand_total) AS revenue FROM orders o WHERE o.date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '3 months' GROUP BY week ORDER BY week
table_schema: Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)

description: RANK() used in outer SELECT but WHERE rank = 1 filter is missing — returns all ranks not just the top
question: What is the top-selling product in each category?
objective: Rank products by SUM(oi.total_price) within each category using RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY c.category_id ORDER BY SUM DESC). Wrap in CTE and filter WHERE rank = 1.
sql: SELECT c.name AS category, p.name AS product, SUM(oi.total_price) AS revenue, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY c.category_id ORDER BY SUM(oi.total_price) DESC) AS rank FROM order_items oi JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id GROUP BY c.category_id, c.name, p.product_id, p.name ORDER BY c.name, rank
table_schema:
Table: order_items (item_id PK, order_id FK->orders, variant_id FK->product_variants, quantity INT, unit_price NUMERIC, total_price NUMERIC)
Table: product_variants (variant_id PK, product_id FK->products, sku VARCHAR, price NUMERIC)
Table: products (product_id PK, category_id FK->categories, name VARCHAR, status VARCHAR)
Table: categories (category_id PK, name VARCHAR, slug VARCHAR)

description: RANK() OVER without PARTITION BY — ranks globally across all cities instead of per-city
question: What is the most popular payment method in each city?
objective: Rank payment methods by COUNT within each city using RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY u.city ORDER BY COUNT DESC). Wrap in CTE and filter WHERE rank = 1.
sql:
WITH ranked AS (
  SELECT u.city, p.method, COUNT(*) AS cnt,
    RANK() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) AS rank
  FROM orders o
  JOIN users u ON o.user_id = u.user_id
  JOIN payments p ON o.order_id = p.order_id
  GROUP BY u.city, p.method
)
SELECT city, method, cnt FROM ranked WHERE rank = 1
table_schema:
Table: orders (order_id PK, user_id FK->users, grand_total NUMERIC, status VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP)
Table: users (user_id PK, full_name VARCHAR, email VARCHAR, city VARCHAR)
Table: payments (payment_id PK, order_id FK->orders, method VARCHAR, status VARCHAR, amount NUMERIC)

description: RANK() result referenced in HAVING instead of a CTE outer WHERE — invalid in PostgreSQL
question: What is the most popular product in each city?
objective: Rank products by COUNT within each city using RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY u.city ORDER BY COUNT DESC). Wrap in CTE and filter WHERE rank = 1 in outer query.
sql: SELECT u.city, p.name AS product, COUNT(oi.item_id) AS order_count, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY u.city ORDER BY COUNT(oi.item_id) DESC) AS rank FROM orders o JOIN users u ON o.user_id = u.user_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY u.city, p.name HAVING rank = 1

Table: product_variants (variant_id PK, product_id FK->products, sku VARCHAR, price NUMERIC)
Table: products (product_id PK, category_id FK->categories, name VARCHAR, status VARCHAR)

question: What is the total revenue by city in the last 30 days?
objective: Join orders and users. SUM(o.grand_total). GROUP BY u.city. Filter o.created_at last 30 days.
sql: SELECT u.city, SUM(o.grand_total) AS total_revenue FROM orders o JOIN users u ON o.user_id = u.user_id WHERE o.created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY u.city ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

sql: SELECT c.name AS category, SUM(oi.total_price) AS total_revenue FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id GROUP BY c.name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

question: What is the total revenue this month?
objective: SUM(o.grand_total) from orders where DATE_TRUNC('month', o.created_at) = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE).
sql: SELECT SUM(grand_total) AS total_revenue FROM orders WHERE DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)

description: Correct RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY city) in CTE with outer WHERE rank = 1
question: What is the most popular payment method in each city?
objective: Rank payment methods by COUNT within each city using RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY u.city). Wrap in CTE and filter WHERE rank = 1.
sql:
WITH ranked AS (
  SELECT u.city, p.method, COUNT(*) AS cnt,
    RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY u.city ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) AS rank
  FROM orders o
  JOIN users u ON o.user_id = u.user_id
  JOIN payments p ON o.order_id = p.order_id
  GROUP BY u.city, p.method
)
SELECT city, method, cnt FROM ranked WHERE rank = 1

description: Correct top-N query with proper join path, SUM(oi.quantity), LIMIT and ORDER BY
question: What are the top 5 products by total units sold?
objective: Join order_items -> product_variants -> products. SUM(oi.quantity). GROUP BY p.name. ORDER BY DESC. LIMIT 5.
sql: SELECT p.name AS product, SUM(oi.quantity) AS units_sold FROM order_items oi JOIN product_variants pv ON oi.variant_id = pv.variant_id JOIN products p ON pv.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.name ORDER BY units_sold DESC LIMIT 5
table_schema:

Table: products (product_id PK, category_id FK->categories, name VARCHAR, status VARCHAR)
